description: j ATLANTA DAILY WORLD Trlday August U 1955 2 Computing Machine Abie To Predict Marriage Outcome WASHINGTON D CPicture if you can the romatic young couple walking up to a machine inserting a coin and knowing within a mat ter of minutes whether their con templated marriage will be a suc cess or failure Fantastic Well maybe not In this era of pushbutton liv ing Howard University has acquir ed a machine which can perdict with a great degree of accuracy whether or not a marriage will be successful The instrument is the HUEAC Howard University Elec tronic Analog Computer Installed last month at a cost of 10500 it is one of the latest of the socalled thinking machines HUEAC does not think however however and Dr Herman R Bran son professor of physics at Howard is quick to admit it The instrument is no better than it operator he says Supply it with the right formulae and it will be 100 per cent accurate Misinform HUEACand shell reciprocate Among the many other benefits resulting from problems now being solved by HUEAC and similar ma chines are smoother riding trains stronger buildings and machinery and better jet engines and pilots
